title: Streblus zeylanicus
intro: Streblus zeylanicus, also known as the Indian coral tree or Nilam, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in Sri Lanka, India, and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Streblus zeylanicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of capsules, tablets, and powders.
taxonomy: Streblus zeylanicus is classified under the family Moraceae, commonly known as the mulberry or fig family. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other members of the Moraceae family. The genus Streblus is a group of tropical trees and shrubs native to various parts of the world, including Asia and Australia. Streblus zeylanicus specifically is native to the Indian subcontinent and parts of Southeast Asia. Within the Moraceae family, Streblus zeylanicus is also part of the tribe Moreae.
uses: Streblus zeylanicus, also known as the 'Pulai' tree, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its leaves, bark, and roots are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the roots of the Streblus zeylanicus tree are used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Streblus zeylanicus is known to have various health benefits. It has been traditionally used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing pain and swelling. Additionally, Streblus zeylanicus has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin problems and respiratory issues. properties: Streblus zeylanicus, commonly known as the Indian fig or Indian horse fig, has been traditionally used for its therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant activity, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The plant's leaves and bark are also believed to have antiseptic and antiviral properties, making it a potential natural remedy for skin infections and other health issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Streblus zeylanicus and its applications in modern medicine.

These compounds may also contribute to the plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, Streblus zeylanicus contains alkaloids and tannins, which can have astringent and antiseptic effects. parts: Streblus zeylanicus, also known as the Black Bead tree, is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark and roots are primarily used in traditional medicine. The bark is known to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The roots of the plant are used to treat fever and other infections. The bark is typically dried and then ground into a powder, which is used to make teas, infusions, and capsules. Some herbalists also use the bark to create tinctures and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
